extra.message: Student.create builds the SQL query using Python string formatting (`%` operator) with the user-controlled `name` parameter, which comes directly from `request.post()` in views.students. This enables SQL injection because the value is concatenated into the query rather than passed as a parameter to cursor.execute().

Jinja2 is initialized with `autoescape=False`. Templates (e.g., course.jinja2 rendering `review.review_text`, `course.description`, `course.title`; students.jinja2 rendering `name` and `error`) render user-controlled data without escaping, allowing stored/reflected XSS.

Passwords are hashed using MD5 without a salt (`md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()`). MD5 is a broken, fast hash unsuitable for password storage and enables easy offline cracking of leaked `pwd_hash` values.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: The CSRF middleware is commented out in the middleware chain (`# csrf_middleware,`), and state-changing POST endpoints (login at `/`, add student, add course, submit review, evaluate, logout) do not validate CSRF tokens. This enables cross-site request forgery.

The Redis-backed session cookie is created with `httponly=False`, making the session cookie accessible to JavaScript via `document.cookie`. Combined with the disabled Jinja2 autoescape, this allows XSS to trivially steal session cookies.

The `students` POST handler creates new students via `Student.create` without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can add arbitrary students. (Similarly, `courses` POST at line 83, `review` POST at line 111, and `evaluate` POST at line 134 lack authentication checks.)

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `courses` POST handler creates courses using user-provided `title` and `description` with no authentication or admin authorization check, despite course management being an administrative function.

The `evaluate` handler creates a mark for a student/course pair without requiring authentication or admin privileges; any anonymous user can evaluate any student, although the UI suggests this is an admin-only feature.

The `review` POST handler writes user-controlled review text to the database with no authentication; anyone can post reviews anonymously. Combined with autoescape=False, this also produces persistent stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The aiohttp Application is instantiated with `debug=True`, which is not appropriate for production and may leak stack traces and other sensitive information in errors.
